title: rosa californica f. nana
intro: Rosa californica f. nana, also known as the Dwarf California Rose, is a fragrant shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating menstrual cramps, and providing relief from digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Rosa californica f. nana include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the flowers, leaves, or roots in hot water or oil.
